source: All-In Podcast — 2025 predictions (bestie guest Gavin Baker)
summary: America could print 5%+ real GDP growth at some point in the next four years due to AI productivity and deregulation
summary_extended: Baker argues that his most contrarian macro belief is that the United States will print at least one year of greater than 5% real GDP growth at some point in the next four years (2025–2028), driven by AI-induced productivity gains combined with a deregulatory policy environment. He draws an explicit analogy to the late 1990s technology boom, which produced 5–6% real GDP growth, and frames the stakes mathematically: 5% real growth doubles the economy every 12 years versus 24 years at 3% growth. The reasoning rests on the premise that AI productivity improvements are now large enough to show up in aggregate economic statistics, and that deregulation will remove friction that has historically suppressed output. Baker acknowledges this is explicitly contrarian and uncertain in timing, but believes the structural conditions are in place for at least one such year within the four-year window. The investment implication is broadly macro — favoring risk assets, cyclicals, and AI-exposed equities — but Baker does not name specific securities in this thesis.
detail: Baker's most contrarian belief: AI-driven productivity gains combined with deregulation could produce late-1990s style growth of 5-6% real GDP, which would double the economy every 12 years vs. 24 years at 3%. He does not specify which year but says 'at least one year' in the next four.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly labels this his most contrarian belief and provides the mathematical framing but acknowledges uncertainty on timing
quote_fragment: America will print at least one year of greater than 5% GDP growth real GDP growth
